mnemonics: Scatter Points Show Correlation (SPSC): Remember that a scatter plot's individual points (S) visually show (S) the potential relationship or correlation (C) between two variables.
keyPoints: A scatter plot displays the relationship between two quantitative variables, with one on the x-axis and the other on the y-axis.
The overall pattern of the plotted points can reveal the direction (positive/negative), form (linear/non-linear), and strength (weak/strong) of the relationship.
Outliers are individual data points that fall outside the overall pattern and should be investigated.
commonMistakes: Assuming correlation implies causation; a relationship shown on a scatter plot does not mean one variable causes the other.
Overplotting: Failing to use techniques like transparency (alpha) or jittering when many points overlap, which can hide the true density and pattern of the data.
